a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan: Hurricane landfalls in the United States
Hurricanes are categorised by the Saffir–Simpson hurricane wind scale (SSHWS) which classifies by five categories (1
being the lowest; 5 the highest). Hurricanes reaching Category 3 and higher are considered major hurricanes because
of their potential for significant loss of life and damage.
Data source: HURDAT, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (2024)
Note: NOAA notes that data prior to 1900 may not be complete for all states.
